_doc: Phase T1.4 (2026-07-11) — pre-dispatch scope guard for AIHarness.send(). Before this, a prompt like 'delete everything on my disk' was classified into a build chip and handed to the CLI agent — no harness-level refusal path existed. Matching semantics mirror reinvention-rules.json: requireAllOf is an AND of OR-groups (case-insensitive substring; every group needs at least one hit). Keep groups NARROW — a destructive verb alone must never match ('delete all the red blocks' is a legit game iterate); it must co-occur with a system-scope phrase. Matched goal → refusal message + abilities list posted to chat, incident logged, NO session dispatch. The Swift side (ScopeGuard.swift) is a generic runner; adding/tuning a group is a content PR, zero rebuild. extraDegenerateFillers EXTENDS the in-binary universal filler set of the trivial-goal guard (e.g. add non-English fillers here).
refusalPreamble: I can't help with that — it's outside what this studio is for.
abilities: Build a mini-game (a puzzle, an arcade game, a card game…)
Build a tool or utility (a timer, a tracker, a converter…)
Create a pipeline that automates a task with your data
Iterate on an app you've already built — just describe the change

groups.refusal: I can't delete or wipe things on your Mac — I only create and edit the apps built here.
I can't build anything that captures or steals credentials.
I can't build malware or attack tools.
I can't change or disable your Mac's system protections.
